sutta: monks there are these 6 tata gata powers of a tatara tata possessed of which the tata gather claims the place of the bull draws the line draw in assemblies and sets a rolling the brahma wheel what 6 here in monks the tattagata knows as it really is the possible as possible and the impossible as impossible in that the tatargata knows this it is the tattagatas the tagatha power whereby the tataagata claims the place of the bull rose the line raw in assemblies and sets a rolling the brahma wheel again the tata gata knows as it really is the result with its possibilities and causes of karmas undertaken in respect of the past come our action undertaken in respect of the past present and future in that he knows this he sets a rolling the brahma wheel he knows the stain purity and emergence in regard to the jhanas liberations and concentrations and attainments remembers many a previous life 1 birth 2 3 etc so forth he remembers each in all its modes and detail with the purified deva eye surpassing the human eye he sees the passing away and re-appearance of beings each according to his karma destroying the asavas that tata gather enters and abides in liberation by mind in liberation by wisdom asava free in that that the tagatha abides it is the tata tas tata power whereby the tata gata claims the place of the bull draws the lion raw in assemblies and sets a rolling the brahma wheel monks these are the 6 and if monks others come and question the tata gather because of his knowledge as it really is of the possible as possible according as the tatar gathers knowledge as it really is of the possible as possible which prevails so that the targatha explains to them by knowledge as it really is when questioned and if others come and question him because of his knowledge as it really is of the result of karma's undertaking the stain purity and emergence in regard to jhana etc previous lives the passing and reappearance of beings or of our destruction according as his knowledge prevails so he explains to them when question now this knowledge as it really is of the possible as possible impossible as impossible i declare to be the to be the possession of the concentrated not of the unconcentrated so too the knowledge as it really is of the other 5 i declare them to be the possession of the concentrated not of the unconcentrated thus verily monks concentration is the way non-concentration the know with the way that's the end of the suta
commentary: here the buddha is first talking about the powers of a tatar gather or a buddha the tata gata means that thus gone 1 this is this 1 of the 10 names of the buddha and here there is given 6 powers of the buddha in some other sutas uh the 10 10 powers of the tata enumerated and the powers here is that he knows the possible as him as possible and the impossible as impossible then he knows the possibilities causes etc of the actions undertaken are kama undertaken and he knows that stain purity and emergence in regard to the janas liberations concentrations and attainments then he remembers previous lives then he has the purified deva eye which you can see the passing away and reappearance of beings and then the last 1 is he has destroyed the asava sin and attains liberation by mind and liberation by wisdom so now the buddha at the end says that these higher knowledges attain because of concentration of samadhi and concentration of samadhi in the sutas is always defined as 1 pointedness of mind or the 4 janas and now the jhanas are synonymous to sati patana because satipattana means intense states of mindfulness and we can see in the [Music] was asked what is the cause of this great psychic power and then he said it is sati patana but we know that psychic power is always due to the jhana so this shows that janas are synonymous with sati patana and also because it is stated that the mark of satipatthana is the mouth of samadhi once a person has samadhi or the jhanas then he automatically has sati patana and that is why in the sutas the buddha said that once you attain sati patana then you abandon the practice because you already it's already automatic the jana is also called the footsteps of the buddha and and here you can see here that the jhanas are declared by the buddha to be very important because the buddha said concentration is the way no concentration the no way in uh pali it is samadhi mago ah samadhi kumago samadi is concentration mago is the way so ah samadhi is no concentration is kumago kumago is a wrong way on here is said to be no way so samadhi is the way no samadhi no way so it's very clear in the buddha's teachings that samadhi is very important um there's no way and no path in the buddha's teachings without samadhi and this also confirmed in the angultranic ayah 4.170 where we heard earlier the variable ananda said there's only 4 ways to attainment of the first 1 is samata first followed by the pasana the second 1 is vipassana followed by samatha the third is together the fourth is meditation on the self until the mind sangha no veneration for the training and no veneration or respect for samadhi so if we don't um if we neglect samadhi and this will cause the disappearance of the true dharma and here it is very clear that the buddha's way 7.60 8 barely monks that among the lighting in company delighted by a company absorbed in the delights of company delighting in gatherings delighted by gatherings absorbed in the delights of gatherings shall find delight alone in seclusion that cannot be and that without finding delight in seclusion he shall grasp the reflection of mind or the characteristic of mind
